mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.5 percent, consumer spending power deteriorates and corporate earnings forecasts are downgraded, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal synchronized consumer demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative median forward returns over 60 trading days in backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indices reflect tightening supply chains and rising capacity utilization, which compresses short-term equity risk premiums and produces positive 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of acceleration episodes across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPI levels above 225.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressures that force markets to price in tighter future monetary policy and erode real corporate margins, resulting in negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in more than 50 percent of high-CPI observations over any 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which supports consumer income growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Median 60-day returns are negative during concurrent payroll gains and unemployment drops in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly correlates with aggregate corporate revenue expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all 3-year rolling windows, or positive GDP growth coincides with negative median forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production indices falling below 90.0 signal severe manufacturing contraction and weakening business investment, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of sub-90.0 observations over a 3-year rolling window.
